excerpt: Reference summaries consistently describe a single-day Karantina massacre with a direct-death range of 600-1,500.
transform_note: Best value is the arithmetic midpoint of the published range for bundle bookkeeping only.
confidence_note: Adequate for a massacre range, but still a secondary synthesis.

inflicting_side: Christian militias with Syrian Army support
excerpt: Retained as the main total-killed range for the siege and massacre combined.
transform_note: Best value is midpoint of the published range; status is kept unknown rather than split.
confidence_note: Useful as a bounded siege total but not status-clean.

victim_side: Camp population and fighters present at the fall of the camp
excerpt: Britannica summarizes the final-day slaughter at about 1,500 killed.
transform_note: Used as corroborative context for the siege-total range, not as a separate estimate row in this bundle.
confidence_note: Reasonable contextual support for massacre-day lethality.

excerpt: Encyclopedia population context indicates a camp population around 15,000 in the mid-1970s.
transform_note: Context only; not used as a denominator because population-in-place during the siege is uncertain.
confidence_note: Helpful for scale, insufficient for DR denominator use.

victim_side: PLO and allied fighters inside Tel al-Zaatar
excerpt: Open-reference summaries note that armed fighters were present inside the camp during the siege.
transform_note: Recorded solely to justify strict unknown-status coding for the main death estimate.
confidence_note: Enough to block a civilian-only estimate using current sources.

victim_side: Palestinian and Lebanese civilians in Sabra and Shatila
excerpt: Analytical summaries consistently characterize the victim pool as overwhelmingly civilian.
transform_note: Used to support civilian status coding for the core death-range estimate.
confidence_note: Strong qualitative support for civilian coding.

victim_side: Palestinian and Lebanese Shiite civilians in Sabra and Shatila
excerpt: The most commonly repeated public range is 1,300-3,500 civilians killed over 16-18 September 1982.
transform_note: Best value is midpoint of the published range; the wide uncertainty is deliberately preserved.
confidence_note: Good public anchor, but too uncertain to over-narrow.

victim_side: Civilian victims in Sabra and Shatila
excerpt: Eyewitness-report collections preserve contemporary body-count context above 2,000, reinforcing the order of magnitude of the massacre.
transform_note: Context only; not used to narrow the main range.
